title: How much time Shinji stayed in neo NERV
display_title: How much time Shinji stayed in neo NERV
wikitext:
[]: How much time Shinji stayed in neo-NERV?             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Jul 05, 2013 11:59 am In the topic of how Shinji could have learned piano so fast, one of the theories is that actually the first piano lesson is an abstraction of Shinji's learning process and the passage of time, meaning that he spent a few weeks inside the place. But the problem with that is that there is various hints that at must only few days have passed between Shinji arriving and the battle in Lilith's Chamber : - Shinji asked for Kaworu to repair his SDAT the day before everything has gone to hell for him(i.e. when Kaworu showed him the outside world), since Shinji plays the damn thing all the time when he has more than one minute with nothing to do, I doubt he waited weeks to ask for Kaworu to fix it. - In the (in)famous "rainbow and horses" duet sequence, Shinji asks Kaworu how he could get better, and Kaworu answer that practice and to repeat is the key. That's not the kind of questions you ask after weeks of quotidian playing which last for most of the day, it's a question for soon after you begin. - Every night he brought to Rei new books with the hope that she will start to read them, the pile at the end suggested that he made this "offering" 4-5 times, and I highly doubt that it took him weeks to find it really odd that Rei don't read them. - When Kaworu and Shinji activated EVA-13, an alarm in the Wunder immediately started to ring, probably because of the DSS Choker that Kaworu was wearing. And since Asuka and Mari arrived almost at the same time that EVA-13 and Mark.09, it means that the Wunder was on stand by around neo-NERV territory, waiting for Gendo to make the first move. I really doubt that WILLE waited for weeks flying around! - Kaworu and Shinji got on a first name basis only when the former convinced the latter to pilot EVA-13, spending weeks being almost the whole day together playing piano and gazing at the stars and only starting to calls themselves by their first name at the end? Unlikely. For me, everything points that Shinji spent only a few days in neo-NERV, besides that would be a nice parallel with NGE, where Kaworu was only around for few days before having to be killed, reinforced the tragedy that they can't be together for more than a few days before tragedy strikes. The creators have pushed the vice to the point of putting Shinji and Kaworu in the official poster for 3.0 respectively under the Vega and Altair star and with the Milkyway running between them, a direct reference to the Tanabata . Besides, wasn't there an interview of the VA that confirmed that the Tanabata symbolism between Shinij and Kaworu was deliberate and that they are fated to only be together for a very short time? Do you have other hints that only a few days passed between Shinji's arrival and the end of the movie? Or maybe hints that much more time passed?

[]:             Quote Post by TruthfulLie » Fri Jul 05, 2013 1:17 pm I've responded to the post in piano thread. To cut to the chase, I think it's probably just few days. Maybe a week or few more days. The idea of abstraction doesn't necessarily equal or suggest weeks have passed. Learning few songs doesn't take weeks of practice, if the goal is to simply hit the right notes. But it's certainly not enough time to learn little nuances and every little details of the piece which is explicitly stated by Shinji himself, wondering how he could play better.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Jul 05, 2013 2:32 pm A week, tops. Just long enough for Shinji to get good at playing bass clef of the piano (which is never that complex).

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Jul 05, 2013 3:45 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: ... bass clef of the piano (which is never that complex).

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Jul 05, 2013 8:51 pm I also agree with a short stay. As little as three or four days in fact. The only major source of delay is that the Wunder appears to have been resupplied in the meantime. Mari and Asuka wear new plugsuits for example.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Fri Jul 05, 2013 9:09 pm Is it really that important how much time passes? If Anno wanted us to know, he would tell us. The answer is "enough time to build a pretty close relationship between Shinji and Kaworu". This is not real life passage of time, this is a film. ...Actually I´m as concerned about this as you, but it seems that Anno doesn´t give a shit, so maybe we should just ignore the time.

[]:             Quote Post by wiser3754 » Fri Jul 05, 2013 10:25 pm My view is that it is a few days, possibly a week at most. The WUNDER had to make it with haste to Tokyo-3 to stop another impact (WILLE knows why NERV wants Shinji, to start impacts) and honestly, the WUNDER having to take weeks to get there just makes it a very slow target. I doubt it was slowed down by any NEMESIS series on the way, as Gendo had anticipated that WILLE would show when the spears were pulled. Come to think of it, awaken EVA-13, kill the last Angels and severely cripple the WUNDER and it's EVA's, very cunning plan Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sat Jul 06, 2013 7:23 am I'd argue a month. I don't have any facts to back that up but I'll stick to my guns. Exactly as long as he was trapped in Unit 01 back in NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by wiser3754 » Sat Jul 06, 2013 6:14 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: I'd argue a month. I don't have any facts to back that up but I'll stick to my guns. Exactly as long as he was trapped in Unit 01 back in NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by Sherbert » Sun Jul 07, 2013 1:17 am Nice analysis on the Tanabata reference. I never noticed the reference myself. Anywho, I also got the impression that Shinji was only with Neo-NERV for a few days. The film's fast pacing only added to that impression.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sun Jul 07, 2013 1:42 am Wow, that Tanabata connection is one of the most interesting things I've learned about the movie. Now that they've been pointed out, I can't not notice Vega and Altair when looking at the 3.0 poster. I guess that whole component would kinda fall flat if Kaworu were to somehow come back in FINAL, right?

[]:             Quote Post by Sherbert » Sun Jul 07, 2013 1:46 am riffraff11235 wrote: I guess that whole component would kinda fall flat if Kaworu were to somehow come back in FINAL, right?

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Sun Jul 07, 2013 6:56 am I think that it was a month like Gendo's papa, at least three weeks. But again, I think it's not that important. We agree that Shinji and Kaworu had a pretty close relationship, and how much time passed? Enough time for that relationship to exist. Everyone gives different answers, a few days, two weeks, a month. That means that theses time periods is what came to us naturally while watching the film, based on the characters, and since the characters are really the important thing, I think the time period shouldn't bother us too much, we won't come to a conclusion anyway. Everyone gets a different feeling while watching the film.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Jul 07, 2013 1:51 pm In NGE they were together no more than three days or so, and it was enough to make their relationship so special. Granted Shinji was close to his absolute lowest and desperately needed some comfort, but even in Q where Shinji was in a far better mental health(at least at the beginning) and their relationship on a more equal foot, Kaworu was still the only beacon of niceness in the completely insane world he ended into and the one who proposed to him a solution to the mark of infamy that supposedly fell on him, so it wouldn't surprise me that a few days passed, it was more than enough to make Kaworu one of the most important people in Shinji's life.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Sun Jul 07, 2013 2:41 pm I wouldn't call awakening after 14 years, something that destroys your life, routine, relationships, etc. something much better than Shinji's situation in episode 24. It's true that knowing about Rei and Eva-01's soul makes everything worse though...

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Jul 07, 2013 3:42 pm You think? He goes beyond his limits and save Rei, and then suddenly awake in a completely foreign ship where everyone is glaring murder at him, Misato don't even look at him or speak to him, and when she does it's with enough coldness to freeze a tropical beach, Asuka whom he didn't even knew if she was alive treats him with a surprising level of aggressiveness... and then he learns that actually 14 years have passed since he saved Rei, that he didn't saved Rei... oh yeah and that he's considered a criminal and that Misato(the closest thing to a mother he had since Yui's death) ordered to put an overpowered bomb around his neck! Then when "Rei" comes, he had to take the decision to leave them, and even if he was angry and from his point of view he has plenty of good reasons to ditch them, he still feels that he betrayed them for Rei and indirectly his father(whom he still pretty much hates). Only once arrived to NERV to find the place completely deserted and in ruins, "Rei" not acting normally and his father only speaking to him once, and it was only to give him orders and disappear. Then comes Kaworu, the only people who interacts with him like an human being and not a subordinate(Gendo), a robot(Rei) or a bunch of jailers(WILLE). Even Sakura, as sweet as she was, still kept a professional distance with him(use of the "-san" suffix, occasionally calls him by his label "BM-03"), so of course Shinji was almost bound to get close to Kaworu. So from here I can easily pictures their bond as being very strong even with only a few days being together.

[]:             Quote Post by Kestrel » Thu Jul 11, 2013 2:46 pm I'm of the opinion that some time was allowed to pass, purposely, so that Shinji would become increasingly depressed and desperate. All part of the G man's plan, of course, to drive Shinji toward a choice or action that would benefit the master plan... Specific factors were intentionally introduced, such as Fuyutski's little chat. Follow this with more isolation, and you've set up a situation where Shinji is given bits of information (with no resolution or answers to inevitable questions) specifically tailored to push him over the edge toward the planned outcome. It's a very nasty piece of manipulation. Gendo at his finest. Even the interactions with Kaworu were likely planned, in set intervals of time...
